Step2 学以致用
The Mystery of Construction of Pyramids 金子塔建造之谜
blocks.headword: The Mystery of Construction of Pyramids

blocks.text: About two million stone blocks were used to build the great pyramid
and they're incredibly massive. The average weight is two and a half tons.
The problem that has puzzled scholars for centuries is how were these
blocks lifted up the height of this massive structure and then fit into place
without the benefit of modem technology. There have been a lot of theories
over the centuries. The oldest recorded one is by the Greek historian
Herodotus. He visited Egypt around 450 B.C. when the pyramid was
already two thousand years old. His theory was that cranes were used much
like we use cranes today to construct tall buildings, and Herodotus may
have seen Egyptians using cranes made of wood. But the problem with this
theory has to do with simple mechanics: a crane needs a wide and sturdy
base to stand on or it will fall over. However, as you get to the top of the
pyramid, there's really no place for a crane to stand. The stone blocks are
too narrow to provide a base.
大约有200万块石块被用来建造大金字塔，并且它们非常地巨大，
平均重量有两吨半。几个世纪以来一直困扰着学者的问题是在没有现
代科技的情况下，这些石块是如何被升到这个高度并安放的。几个世
纪以来已经有很多理论，而有记录的最古老的理论是由希腊历史学家
赫罗多斯提出的。他在公元前
450年来到埃及，那时金字塔已经存在两千多年了。他的理论和我
们今天使用吊车来建造高楼一样，那时使用的也是吊车，并且那时赫
罗多斯可能看到了埃及人使用木质的吊车。但这个理论在简单力学方
面存在问题：吊车需要宽阔而坚实的地基来安放否则就会倒塌。但当
你到金子塔顶端的时候，真的就没有安放吊车的地方了。石块太窄了
因此不能用作地基。
